place: East Meadow New York, New York

facts.text: East Meadow got its name in 1655 when surveyors noted the area was ideal for grazing cattle and later sheep, supplying over half the nation's wool.
The Barnum farm, once a sprawling estate, later became the site of Barnum Woods Elementary School after the Hoeffner family rented it in 1914.
East Meadow Public Library, a community hub, features a bronze statue of the Bicentennial Committee outside its entrance.
Merrick Avenue, now a busy thoroughfare, was originally Barnum Avenue and once ran past the first school on Front Street.
Eisenhower Park hosts cultural nights, free concerts, and the annual Carltun, drawing residents for community celebrations.
In June 2024, East Meadow welcomed eight ICC Men's T20 World Cup cricket matches, a first for the hamlet.
